medium.summary: Title theme echoes Greenhalgh 'How to Read a Paper' series; body is original stroke time-pressure protocol, not a handbook paste.
High-level paraphrases of GRADE/QUADAS-2/STARD/TRIPOD/PROBAST/RoB2/PRISMA domains without full official item dumps; residual handbook-overlap concern only.

medium.location: CEA/NASCET-ACAS absolute-risk bridge; DAPT-style synthetic meta
medium.summary: Approximate public trial magnitudes used in original teaching arithmetic; not verbatim paper Table 1 pastes.
Uniform dense pedagogical voice (likely LLM-assisted) creates residual training-data-echo uncertainty without identifiable commercial paste.

low.summary: Shared scientific terms and formulas (ARR, NNT, PICO, DAG, ITT, mRS, LR, AUROC, DCA) — not copyrightable alone.
Nominative trial acronyms (NINDS, ECASS III, DAWN, POINT, CHANCE, etc.) with learner-side extraction tasks rather than embedded primary tables.
Named epidemiologic concepts (Table 1 Fallacy, immortal time, colliders) taught via original stroke vignettes.
Standard heuristics (dichotomization information loss; Pauker-Kassirer thresholds) paraphrased.

safe_signals: Web Edition original-teaching disclaimer on all 14 chapters
Footer: original CRIT-APP figures/tables; methods standards cited by name only
Synthetic vignettes: REPERFUSE-2/3, Neuromab, DeepVessel-Net, RecoveryForest, fictional claims EVT paper
Original frameworks: DFR, F&T-First, 20-minute protocol, Absolute Effect Translation Checklist
No reproduced-with-permission, ISBN, abstract paste, or publisher boilerplate found
